lede: A look at June 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lil Bub and Jussie Smollett, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hot air balloon
events.desc: A hot air balloon catches fire mid-flight and crashes in Praia Grande, Santa Catarina, Brazil, killing 8 of the 21 on board.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: The U.S. Supreme Court rules in Texas v. Johnson, 491 U.S. 397, that American flag-burning is a form of political protest protected by the First Amendment.

events.title: Primer Congreso del Hombre Andino
events.desc: The Primer Congreso del Hombre Andino is inaugurated in Arica, Chile.

events.title: Ellen Fairclough
events.desc: Ellen Fairclough is sworn in as Canada's first female Cabinet Minister.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Italy begins an unsuccessful invasion of France.

events.title: Ludwig von Reuter
events.desc: Admiral Ludwig von Reuter scuttles the German fleet at Scapa Flow, Orkney. The nine sailors killed are the last casualties of World War I.

events.title: Capture of Guam
events.desc: The United States captures Guam from Spain. The few warning shots fired by the U.S. naval vessels are misinterpreted as salutes by the Spanish garrison, which was unaware that the two nations were at war.

events.title: Wallachian Revolution of 1848
events.desc: In the Wallachian Revolution, Ion Heliade Rădulescu and Christian Tell issue the Proclamation of Islaz and create a new republican government.

events.title: Greek War of Independence
events.desc: Greek War of Independence: Egyptian forces capture Psara in the Aegean Sea.

events.title: New Hampshire
events.desc: New Hampshire becomes the ninth state to ratify the Constitution of the United States.

births.name: Charles Momsen
births.line: American admiral, invented the Momsen lung (died 1967)

deaths.text: Frederick Crews, American essayist and literary critic
Sukarno, Indonesian engineer and politician, 1st President of Indonesia
Bertha von Suttner, Austrian journalist and author, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Father's Day (Egypt, Lebanon, Jordan, Syria, Uganda, Pakistan, United Arab Emirates)
